title: dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. siciliensis
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. siciliensis, also known as the Sicilian Marsh Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the island of Sicily in Italy. It is a terrestrial orchid characterized by its short stems and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. siciliensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including hypertension and digestive problems.
